replication_study.hypothesis: Participants' contributions to the shared account (cont) will be significantly lower in rounds where the outcome distribution includes loss prospects (losses = 1) compared to rounds with no loss prospects (losses = 0), controlling for clustering at participant (id) and session (sessioncode) levels.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: McCarter_OrgBehavior_2010_pILK data CLEAN.csv (oTree pILK replication dataset)
data_plan.source_type: Laboratory/online experimental dataset (oTree-based threshold public goods game)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Step-level public goods game with threshold 300 and four outcome variance conditions: 600, (400, 800), (200, 1000), (0, 1200). Loss-prospect indicator: losses (0/1).
data_plan.access_details: Provided in repository; no external restrictions. Data assumed available at /app/data during execution.

qualification.explanation: This dataset reproduces the same task structure (step-level public goods dilemma) and the critical manipulation of outcome distributions that include gains-only vs. losses-and-gains prospects. It provides an independent and larger sample collected under similar conditions (lab/online), allowing a robust test of the original focal claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original paper details the step-level public goods game manipulation and hypothesis (original_paper.pdf, Study 1 Methods and Hypotheses 1–2). The replication dataset includes: cont (contribution 0–125), BONUS categories corresponding to outcome distributions {600; (400,800); (200,1000); (0,1200)}, and a computed losses indicator (losses) (McCarter_OrgBehavior_2010_pILK _dictionary.pdf). The focal claim and statistical result (F(1,61)=4.73, p<.05) are stated in initial_details.txt and summarized in post_registration.json (Study 1 numerical results).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original Study 1 used three thresholds (low=160, medium=300, high=440) and analyzed contributions via repeated-measures ANOVA controlling for threshold (original_paper.pdf; post_registration.json). The replication dataset fixes the threshold at 300 only (McCarter_OrgBehavior_2010_pILK _dictionary.pdf: sessionconfigthreshold=300), so it does not vary threshold across games. The replication sample is larger (N≈320 vs N=62 after exclusions in original, post_registration.json) and includes sessions identified as lab/Prolific/Qualtrics (dictionary: sessionconfigname). Games are organized into four rounds mapped to bonus distributions (dictionary: BONUS and round), not 12 games across 3 thresholds × 4 variance levels.

data_plan.notes: Variables: cont (0–125), losses (0/1), BONUS (string-coded categories), id (1–320), sessioncode, round (1–4). Some survey items (loss_aversion, fear) have missing values, handled via listwise deletion per model. sessionconfigshow_consent_form and gender_self_describe have substantial missingness but are not used. BONUS is string-coded; losses provides the binary prospect classification for analysis. All I/O paths for analysis must be under /app/data.

steps: Verify presence of key variables: cont, losses, id, sessioncode; coerce to appropriate types.
Compute descriptive statistics and group means of cont by losses.
Fit mixed-effects linear model: cont ~ losses with random intercepts for participant (id) and session (sessioncode) to mirror the Stata mixed specification.
Secondary analyses: Fit analogous mixed models for loss_aversion and fear on losses (if present) to test ancillary mechanisms.
Export a text summary and a machine-readable JSON of model results to /app/data.
Compare the sign and significance of the losses coefficient to the original reported effect and mean differences.
planned_method.models: Linear mixed-effects model (statsmodels MixedLM) with fixed effect for losses and random intercepts for participant (id) and variance components for session (sessioncode).
planned_method.independent_variables: losses (1 if outcome distribution includes losses-and-gains prospects, 0 otherwise).
planned_method.control_variables: Random intercepts for participant (id) and session (sessioncode) to account for within-subject and within-session dependence; no additional fixed controls (mirrors Stata .do).
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10+; pandas, numpy, statsmodels, scipy, patsy.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient on losses in cont ~ losses mixed-effects model (expected negative).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z-test (t-test equivalent) on the losses coefficient; two-tailed, alpha=0.05.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for variables involved in each model (e.g., drop rows with NA in cont/losses/id/sessioncode for the main model; drop NA in each secondary DV for their respective models).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary focal test is a single coefficient (losses) on cont. Secondary models (loss_aversion, fear) are exploratory; no multiplicity adjustment planned for the focal test.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Statistical significance at p < 0.05 (two-tailed) and directionality consistent with the hypothesis (losses coefficient < 0).

files.McCarter_OrgBehavior_2010_pILK _analysis: Python rewrite of the provided Stata .do file. Reads /app/data/McCarter_OrgBehavior_2010_pILK data CLEAN.csv, fits MixedLM models: cont ~ losses with random intercepts for id and session (via variance components), and optionally loss_aversion ~ losses and fear ~ losses. Writes results to /app/data/mccarter_pILK_mixedlm_results.txt and /app/data/mccarter_pILK_mixedlm_results.json.

codebase.notes: All I/O uses /app/data. The Python script preserves the original model logic from the Stata .do: mixed cont losses || sessioncode: || id:. The session random effects are implemented via variance components (vc_formula) in statsmodels. Execution requires the dataset to be present at /app/data with the exact filename. No other scripts are needed.

instructions: Ensure the dataset is available at: /app/data/McCarter_OrgBehavior_2010_pILK data CLEAN.csv
comparison_metrics: Sign and p-value of the losses coefficient (expected negative and p < 0.05), compared to original F(1,61)=4.73, p<.05 (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf Study 1).
Mean contributions (cont) by losses (0 vs 1) to assess direction and magnitude of difference.
Convergence status of mixed models and robustness across sessions/participants.
